title: SPARC Serotonin (5-HT) Immunohistochemistry Protocol in Rat Tissues Labeled with Cholera Toxin B-fragment
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"frrvu","text":"This protocol describes the immunofluorescent labeling technique used to identify serotonin expression in CtB-labelled phrenic motor neurons and within a defined region of interest surrounding phrenic motor neurons.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Day 1: primary antibodies required: 5-HT: Rabbit anti-5-HT (Immunostar #20080) Cholera toxin B-fragment: Goat anti-CT-B (Millipore #227040)
Place 40um transverse spinal cord sections into 1xPBS-Triton (0.1%) in 12 well cell culture plates
5x washes in 1xPBS-Triton (0.1%) for 5 minutes each at room temperature
Antigen retrieval: place tissues into Heat-Induced Epitope Retrieval (TissuePro, Cat#: HIER01-32R) for 30 minutes at 85 degrees C
5x washes in 1xPBS-Triton (0.1%) for 5 minutes each at room temperature
Blocking: place tissues in 5% Normal Donkey Serum (NDS) in 1xPBS-Triton (0.1%) for 60 minutes at room temperature
Primary Antibody Incubation: Incubate tissues in: 5%NDS in 1xPBS-Triton (0.1%), Rabbit anti-5-HT (1:2000), and Goat anti-Ct-B (1:2500) for 1 hour at room temperature
Continue primary antibody incubation overnight at 4 degrees C
Day 2: secondary antibodies required: AlexaFluor 488: donkey anti-goat (Invitrogen, Ref#A11055) AlexaFluor 594: donkey anti-rabbit (Invitrogen, Ref#A21207)
5x washes in 1xPBS-Triton (0.1%) for 5 minutes each at room temperature
Secondary Antibody Incubation: Incubate tissues in: 5%NDS in 1xPBS-Triton (0.1%), donkey anti-goat (1:1000), and donkey anti-rabbit (1:500) for 2 hours at room temperature
5x washes in 1xPBS for 5 minutes each at room temperature
Mount tissues on Superfrost Plus microscope slides
Coverslip with VectaShield Antifade Hard Set Mounting Medium (Cat#:H-1400)
creator: Elisa Gonzalez-Rothi
